apple black rot: Apple black rot typically appears as circular, sunken, black lesions on the fruit, surrounded by a velvety greenish-black spore mass.
apple mosaic virus: Apple mosaic virus causes green and yellow mosaic patterns on leaves, which may distort and curl them.
apple rust: Apple rust appears as bright orange or yellowish-orange spots on the leaves and fruit of infected trees.
apple scab: Apple scab is characterized by raised, dark-colored lesions on the fruit's surface, resembling scabs.
banana panama disease: Banana Panama disease causes wilting, yellowing of leaves, and decay of the plant's vascular system.
basil downy mildew: Basil downy mildew appears as yellowing and browning of leaves with fuzzy gray or purple spores.
bean halo blight: Bean halo blight appears as small, yellowish halos surrounding dark-brown spots on the leaves and pods.
bean mosaic virus: Bean mosaic virus causes mottled and distorted leaves in bean plants with distinct mosaic-like patterns.
bean rust: Bean rust appears as reddish-brown pustules on the leaves and stems of infected plants.
bell pepper leaf spot: Bell pepper leaf spot is characterized by circular, dark brown lesions with yellow halos on the leaves.
blueberry rust: Blueberry rust appears as bright orange pustules on the undersides of the leaves.
broccoli downy mildew: Broccoli downy mildew appears as yellow or pale green spots that eventually turn brown or black.
cabbage alternaria leaf spot: Cabbage alternaria leaf spot appears as dark brown to black spots with concentric rings on the leaves.
carrot cavity spot: Carrot cavity spot appears as small, dark, sunken lesions on the root surface.
cauliflower alternaria leaf spot: Cauliflower alternaria leaf spot appears as circular lesions with brown centers and yellow halos on the leaves.
celery anthracnose: Celery anthracnose appears as small, sunken, dark lesions on the leaves, stems, and petioles.
celery early blight: Celery early blight displays small, dark brown, irregularly shaped lesions on the leaves and stems.
cherry leaf spot: Cherry leaf spot appears as small, circular and dark spots on the leaves of cherry trees.
cherry powdery mildew: Cherry powdery mildew appears as white, powdery patches on the leaves, stems, and fruit of cherry trees.
citrus canker: Citrus canker appears as raised, corky lesions on citrus fruit, leaves, and stems.
citrus greening disease: Citrus greening disease is characterized by blotchy mottling and yellowing of leaves, stunted growth, and misshapen fruit.
coffee leaf rust: Coffee leaf rust appears as yellow-orange powdery spots on the undersides of coffee plant leaves.
corn gray leaf spot: Corn gray leaf spot appears as small gray to tan lesions with dark borders on the leaves.
corn northern leaf blight: Corn northern leaf blight appears as elongated, cigar-shaped lesions with tan centers and dark borders on corn leaves.
corn rust: Corn rust appears as orange or reddish-brown pustules on the leaves, stems, and husks of infected plants.
corn smut: Corn smut appears as large, gray-black, distorted galls or tumors on the ears, leaves, and stalks of corn plants.
cucumber angular leaf spot: Cucumber angular leaf spot appears as small, water-soaked lesions that develop into angular, yellowish-brown spots on leaves.
cucumber bacterial wilt: Cucumber bacterial wilt appears as wilting and yellowing leaves, with brown vascular discoloration in the stems.
cucumber powdery mildew: Cucumber powdery mildew appears as white powdery patches on the leaves and stems of the plant.
eggplant cercospora leaf spot: Circular or oval-shaped purple or brown spots with yellow halos on the leaves of eggplant.
garlic leaf blight: Garlic leaf blight appears as brown and yellow lesions on the leaves, eventually leading to their withering.
garlic rust: Garlic rust is characterized by orange pustules or lesions on the leaves and stems of the plant.
ginger leaf spot: Ginger leaf spot appears as small, dark brown to black spots on the leaves.
ginger sheath blight: Ginger sheath blight appears as dark brown lesions with rotting tissue surrounded by a white cottony mycelium.
grape black rot: Grape black rot appears as small black circular lesions on the fruit, leaves, and stems.
grape downy mildew: Grape downy mildew appears as fuzzy, grayish-white patches on the undersides of leaves.
grape leaf spot: Grape leaf spot appears as small, dark, circular lesions with a yellow halo on the leaves.
grapevine leafroll disease: Grapevine leafroll disease causes infected leaves to exhibit a reddish discoloration along the leaf veins.
lettuce downy mildew: Lettuce downy mildew appears as yellowish-green patches on the upper surface of leaves, with a fuzzy grayish growth on the underside.
lettuce mosaic virus: Lettuce mosaic virus causes mosaic patterns on the leaves with light and dark green patches.
maple tar spot: Maple tar spot appears as black, tar-like raised spots on the leaves of maple trees.
peach leaf curl: Peach leaf curl causes red, curled, and distorted leaves with a blister-like appearance on peach trees.
plum pocket disease: Plum pocket disease is characterized by swollen, malformed, and hollowed-out fruit with a powdery grey appearance.
potato early blight: Potato early blight shows distinct concentric rings with dark brown lesions on leaves and stems.
potato late blight: Potato late blight appears as dark, water-soaked lesions on leaves, stems, and tubers.
rice blast: Rice blast appears as small, oval lesions with gray centers and brown borders on rice leaves.
rice sheath blight: Rice sheath blight appears as elongated lesions with a dark brown border and white cottony growth.
squash powdery mildew: Squash powdery mildew appears as white, powdery spots or patches on the leaves and stems.
strawberry anthracnose: Strawberry anthracnose appears as dark, sunken lesions on the fruits, leaves, and stems of the plant.
strawberry leaf scorch: Strawberry leaf scorch appears as reddish-brown necrotic lesions that often have a purple border.
tobacco mosaic virus: Tobacco mosaic virus causes distinct light and dark green mottling or mosaics on the leaves.
tomato bacterial leaf spot: Tomato bacterial leaf spot appears as dark, water-soaked lesions surrounded by a yellow halo on the leaves.
tomato early blight: Tomato early blight appears as brown, concentric lesions with a darker center surrounded by a yellow halo.
tomato late blight: Tomato late blight appears as dark, water-soaked lesions on leaves, stems, and fruits, often covered with a fuzzy, white mold.
tomato leaf mold: Tomato leaf mold appears as yellowish-green spots or patches with fuzzy gray or brown mold on the underside.
tomato mosaic virus: Tomato mosaic virus causes distinct mottled patterns of light and dark green on tomato leaves.
tomato septoria leaf spot: Tomato septoria leaf spot appears as numerous small, dark brown spots with yellow halos on leaves.
tomato yellow leaf curl virus: Tomato yellow leaf curl virus causes yellowing and curling of tomato leaves with stunted plant growth.
zucchini yellow mosaic virus: Zucchini yellow mosaic virus causes mottled yellowing and mosaic patterns on the leaves of infected plants.
